input: At the moment at the transaction, I needed to pay the VAT. But at the time of the customs clearance I needed to pay it again. How can I proceed to get a refund of the VAT paid to the seller?
ai_suggestion:
If you've paid VAT twice—once at the transaction and again during customs clearance—you can request a refund for the VAT paid at checkout. Here’s how you can proceed:

- **Keep Proof of Payment**: Ensure you have all the necessary documentation showing the VAT payments made.
- **Request a Refund**: You can request a VAT refund on a completed order. Make sure to contact Catawiki with your proof of payment to initiate the refund process.

For more detailed guidance, you can refer to the [Do I have to pay import tax to Catawiki?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-custom-fees/do-i-have-to-pay-import-tax-to-catawiki) article.
gpt4_output:
If you've paid VAT twice—once at the transaction and again during customs clearance—you can request a refund for the VAT paid at checkout. Here’s how you can proceed:

- **Keep Proof of Payment**: Ensure you have all the necessary documentation showing the VAT payments made.
- **Request a Refund**: You can request a VAT refund on a completed order. Make sure to contact Catawiki with your proof of payment to initiate the refund process.

For more detailed guidance, you can refer to the [Do I have to pay import tax to Catawiki?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-custom-fees/do-i-have-to-pay-import-tax-to-catawiki) article.
expected_output:
If you were charged import VAT at checkout and then asked to pay VAT again during customs, you can request a VAT refund on a completed order. We generally transfer import tax collected at checkout to the relevant tax authorities, so you shouldn’t be charged twice; if you were, keep proof of the customs payment and any invoices or receipts to support your request. 

To request a refund, follow these steps:
- Gather proof: customs receipt, payment reference and the commercial/shipping invoice.
- Open the completed order page and use the contact button to explain that you paid import VAT twice, attaching all proof.
- We’ll review the evidence and assist with a VAT refund where applicable.
Providing both the checkout and customs receipts will speed up the review. 

Read the Help Centre articles for more details: [Do I have to pay import tax to Catawiki?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-custom-fees/do-i-have-to-pay-import-tax-to-catawiki) and [How to use Catawiki’s tax numbers for shipping](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/seller-custom-fees/how-to-use-catawikis-tax-numbers-for-shipping).

input: Non ho ricevuto il buono per la promozione di articoli venduti
ai_suggestion:
Sembra che tu non abbia ricevuto il buono per la promozione degli articoli venduti. Ci sono diverse ragioni per cui un buono potrebbe non funzionare o non essere ricevuto:

- **Non hai raggiunto il minimo richiesto**: Se il tuo buono richiede un'offerta finale minima, devi assicurarti che l'offerta finale sia pari o superiore al minimo richiesto.
- **Buono scaduto**: Non puoi utilizzare un buono che è scaduto.
- **Codice errato**: Assicurati che il codice inserito corrisponda esattamente a quello ricevuto, inclusi eventuali caratteri speciali o punteggiatura.
- **Non collegato all'account corretto**: Se un buono è collegato al tuo account, non potrai utilizzarlo su un altro account.
- **Tentativo di utilizzo di un secondo buono**: Puoi utilizzare solo un buono per ordine.
- **Buono già utilizzato**: Puoi utilizzare un buono solo una volta, anche se il totale dell'ordine è inferiore al valore del buono.

Se nessuna di queste condizioni si applica e stai ancora riscontrando problemi, ti consigliamo di contattarci prima di effettuare il pagamento, poiché non possiamo aggiungere un buono a un ordine già pagato.
gpt4_output:
It looks like you didn't receive the coupon for the promotion on the items sold. There are several reasons why a voucher may not work or you may not receive it:

- **You did not meet the minimum requirement**: If your voucher requires a minimum final bid, you must ensure that your final bid is equal to or greater than the minimum required.
- **Expired voucher**: You cannot use a voucher that has expired.
- **Incorrect code**: Please ensure that the code you entered exactly matches the one you received, including any special characters or punctuation.
- **Not linked to the correct account**: If a voucher is linked to your account, you will not be able to use it on another account.
- **Attempting to use a second voucher**: You can only use one voucher per order.
- **Voucher already used**: You can only use a voucher once, even if the order total is less than the value of the voucher.

If none of these conditions apply and you are still experiencing issues, we recommend contacting us before making payment, as we cannot add a voucher to an order that has already been paid for.
expected_output:
Vouchers for promotions are usually linked to your account and either appear automatically at checkout or must be added there. They also have conditions that can prevent use — for example an expiry date, a minimum final bid, single‑use only, or only one voucher per order — and the code must be entered exactly as sent. Check for emails or notifications (including your spam folder) where we confirm vouchers and instructions.   

Try these steps to find the coupon and make it work:
- Sign in to the account you used to sell and check Notifications and Emails for the voucher.
- Go to checkout for the relevant order — vouchers linked to your account usually show there; if not, use ‘Add voucher’ and enter the code exactly.
- Make sure the object’s final bid meets any minimum and the voucher hasn’t expired or already been used.
- If none of the above apply, contact our Customer Support team before you pay so we can check your voucher. 

Read more in the Help Centre: [How do I activate my voucher code](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/vouchers/how-do-i-activate-my-voucher-code) and [Why isn’t my voucher working?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/vouchers/i-can-t-use-my-voucher)
user_question: I did not receive the coupon for the promotion of sold items

input: Un gioiello prodotto in laboratorio e non personalizzato, venduto da un venditore PRO e spedito in Italia, è coperto da diritto di recesso?
ai_suggestion: I gioielli prodotti in laboratorio e venduti da un venditore PRO sono generalmente coperti dal diritto di recesso, a meno che non rientrino in specifiche esclusioni. Ecco i punti principali da considerare:\n\n- **Diritto di recesso**: Se sei un consumatore nell'UE, hai il diritto di recedere dall'acquisto entro 14 giorni dalla ricezione dell'oggetto, senza dover fornire una motivazione. Questo periodo è noto come \
gpt4_output: Lab-made jewelry sold by a PRO seller is generally covered by the right of withdrawal, unless it falls under specific exclusions. Here are the main points to consider:\n\n- **Right of withdrawal**: If you are a consumer in the EU, you have the right to withdraw from your purchase within 14 days of receiving the item, without having to provide a reason. This period is known as the \
expected_output:
Yes — as an EU consumer buying from a PRO seller with the object shipped to Italy, you’re generally entitled to the right of withdrawal: a 14‑day cooling‑off period starting the day after delivery, provided the object isn’t excluded by law or our policy. The seller must be marked ‘Pro’ on the object page and you must notify the seller or Catawiki within those 14 days  .

Check these key points before you decide to withdraw:
- Check whether the object contains diamonds, gold or silver — objects whose price is linked to market fluctuations (for example diamonds) are excluded from the right of withdrawal .  
- The object must not be personalised or made to your specifications to be eligible .  
- If eligible, notify within 14 days, return the object in its original condition within the approved period, and expect to pay return shipping; follow the return steps we describe .

Read more in the Help Centre: [I want to cancel my purchase](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-order-issues/i-want-to-cancel-my-purchase) and [Policies: EU & UK consumer rights policy](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-terms/policies-eu-uk-consumer-rights-policy).
user_question: Is a piece of jewelry made in a laboratory and not personalized, sold by a PRO seller and shipped to Italy, covered by the right of withdrawal?

Your right of withdrawal depends on who you bought from and the type of object. If you’re a consumer in the EU/EEA or the UK and you bought the object from a professional seller (look for the ‘Pro’ label), you may have a 14‑day cooling‑off right; purchases from private sellers are not covered . Certain object types are excluded — for example goods linked to financial markets, custom‑made items, quickly decaying goods and sealed media with broken seals .

To check whether your purchase is covered, do the following:
- Look at the object page for the seller status (‘Pro’ or ‘Private’) and read the object description for any exclusions.   
- Note the delivery date — the 14‑day cooling‑off period starts the day after you receive the object; you must notify us within those 14 days to withdraw.   
- Examine the object only as you would in a shop, keep packaging and take care (you may be liable for diminished value). If you decide to withdraw, notify our Customer Support team or use the model withdrawal form and return the object if we approve the request; return shipping is usually your responsibility. 

For full details, read Policies: EU & UK consumer rights policy and I want to cancel my purchase in the Help Centre.  
[Policies: EU & UK consumer rights policy](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-terms/policies-eu-uk-consumer-rights-policy) [I want to cancel my purchase](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-order-issues/i-want-to-cancel-my-purchase)

input: Quando nei dettagli c'è scritto che è un'edizione originale cosa si intende?
ai_suggestion: Quando nei dettagli di un oggetto su Catawiki si indica che è un'edizione originale, si intende che si tratta della prima stampa o pubblicazione autentica di quel libro o opera, non una ristampa o una copia successiva. Queste edizioni originali sono spesso molto ricercate dai collezionisti perché rappresentano la versione autentica e storicamente significativa dell'opera. Ad esempio, un'edizione originale può essere una stampa storica ben conservata, con caratteristiche tipografiche e illustrazioni originali, come nel caso di alcune edizioni antiche di opere famose, che possono includere dettagli come ritratti, incisioni su legno e marchi tipografici originali.\n\nIn sintesi, \
gpt4_output:
When an item's details on Catawiki indicate that it is an original edition, it means it is the authentic first printing or publication of that book or work, not a reprint or later copy. These original editions are often highly sought after by collectors because they represent the authentic and historically significant version of the work. For example, an original edition may be a well-preserved historical print, with original typographical features and illustrations, as is the case with some early editions of famous works, which may include details such as portraits, woodcuts, and original printer's marks.

In summary,
expected_output:
When a listing’s details say an object is an ‘original edition’, we expect the seller to identify the edition and publishing information rather than a later reprint. The Help Centre explains that first editions, later editions and reprints are distinct and should be described with the publishing date and edition number (or whether it’s part of a limited edition) so bidders can judge rarity and authenticity  .

To make this clear in a listing:
- Add edition details: publishing date, edition number and ‘limited’ status where applicable .
- Show the title page, colophon/verso and full covers; include all pages for books and close-ups of edition numbers or signatures .
- State provenance or certificates, and note condition using the grading guidance in the books guidelines .

Read our Help Centre page [Books submission guidelines](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/specific-guidelines-books-historical-memorabilia/books-submission-guidelines) for full guidance on edition info, photos, provenance and examples of accepted objects.

input: Come effettuare la spedizione di un oggetto venduto
ai_suggestion:
Per spedire un oggetto venduto su Catawiki, segui questi passaggi:

- **Conferma del pagamento**: Aspetta che il pagamento dell'acquirente sia confermato. Ti informeremo quando sarà il momento di spedire l'oggetto.

- **Preparazione della spedizione**:
  - Usa un servizio di spedizione che fornisca un codice di tracciamento valido.
  - Imballa l'oggetto con cura per evitare danni durante il trasporto.
  - Considera l'uso di un'assicurazione di spedizione per proteggere l'oggetto in caso di perdita o danno.

- **Spedizione**:
  - Spedisci l'oggetto entro 3 giorni lavorativi dalla conferma del pagamento.
  - Comunica con l'acquirente tramite il sistema di messaggistica interno per organizzare la spedizione o il ritiro.

- **Responsabilità**: Sei responsabile dell'oggetto fino a quando non viene consegnato all'acquirente. In caso di problemi durante la spedizione, contatta direttamente la compagnia di spedizioni per avviare un'indagine.

Per ulteriori dettagli, puoi consultare l'articolo [Shipping your object](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/seller-shipping-costs/can-i-ship-multiple-objects-together-to-the-same-buyer).
gpt4_output:
To ship an item sold on Catawiki, follow these steps:

- **Payment confirmation**: Wait for the buyer's payment to be confirmed. We will notify you when it's time to ship the item.

- **Shipping preparation**:
- Use a shipping service that provides a valid tracking number.
- Package the item carefully to avoid damage during transportation.
- Consider using shipping insurance to protect the item in case of loss or damage.

- **Shipping**:
- Ship the item within 3 business days of payment confirmation.
- Communicate with the buyer via the internal messaging system to arrange shipping or pickup.

- **Liability**: You are responsible for the item until it is delivered to the buyer. If you encounter any shipping issues, please contact the shipping company directly to investigate.

For more details, please see the article [Shipping your object](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/seller-shipping-costs/can-i-ship-multiple-objects-together-to-the-same-buyer).
expected_output:
When an object sells we’ll notify you as soon as the buyer has paid; unless the buyer will pick it up, you must ship the object within three working days from payment. You remain responsible for the object until delivery, so always use a valid Track & Trace and consider registered, insured shipping appropriate to the object’s value. Smart shipping can simplify label generation and customs for international orders. We are not the shipping party and cannot be liable for transport damage.  

To ship a sold object, follow these steps:
- Confirm the buyer’s address via the order page and agree delivery or pickup.   
- Package the object securely and use registered/insured shipping for valuable objects.   
- For international orders, add customs information and print the required forms; smart shipping can pre‑fill and guide you.    
- Hand the package to the shipping company, add the Track & Trace on the order page and mark the order as shipped; if you can’t ship within three working days, message the buyer. 

For full details and step‑by‑step instructions on timing, tracking, customs and smart shipping, see the Help Centre article [When should I ship my lot](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/shipment-preparation/when-should-i-ship-my-lot).
user_question: How to ship a sold item

- **Controleer of je account geactiveerd is**: Klik op de link in de bevestigingsmail die je van ons hebt ontvangen bij het registreren.

Als je na deze stappen nog steeds problemen ondervindt, kan het zijn dat je account is geschorst vanwege een schending van de voorwaarden. In dat geval heb je een e-mail ontvangen met uitleg hierover.

Try deleting your cookies and trying to log in again. If you're still having trouble after trying these steps, your account may have been suspended for a violation of the Terms of Service. input: Kan verzendinglabel niet afprinten Error 404
ai_suggestion:
Het lijkt erop dat je een probleem hebt met het afdrukken van je verzendlabel vanwege een Error 404. Dit kan te maken hebben met een technische storing of een probleem met de website. Hier zijn enkele stappen die je kunt proberen om het probleem op te lossen:

- **Controleer je internetverbinding**: Zorg ervoor dat je een stabiele internetverbinding hebt.
- **Probeer een andere browser**: Soms kan het probleem worden opgelost door een andere webbrowser te gebruiken.
- **Vernieuw de pagina**: Probeer de pagina te vernieuwen of opnieuw te laden.
- **Controleer de URL**: Zorg ervoor dat de URL correct is en geen typefouten bevat.
- **Probeer het later opnieuw**: Het kan een tijdelijk probleem zijn, dus probeer het later nog eens.

Als deze stappen het probleem niet oplossen, raad ik aan om de [Catawiki Help-pagina](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/shipping-status/shipping-exception-statuses-what-they-mean-and-what-to-do) te bezoeken voor meer informatie over verzendproblemen en mogelijke oplossingen.
gpt4_output:
It looks like you're having trouble printing your shipping label due to an Error 404. This could be due to a technical glitch or a website issue. Here are some steps you can try to fix the issue:

- **Check your internet connection**: Make sure you have a stable internet connection.
- **Try a different browser**: Sometimes using a different web browser can solve the issue.
- **Refresh the page**: Try refreshing or reloading the page.
- **Check the URL**: Make sure the URL is correct and doesn't have typos.
- **Try again later**: It might be a temporary issue, so try again later.

If these steps don't solve the problem, I recommend visiting the [Catawiki Help page](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/shipping-status/shipping-exception-statuses-what-they-mean-and-what-to-do) for more information about shipping issues and possible solutions.
expected_output:
If you see an Error 404 when trying to print a shipping label, the Help Centre explains that labels and customs forms are available to download from the relevant order page or from the confirmation email you received after the sale. For smart shipping we usually create both the shipping label and any customs forms for you to print; if no customs forms appear, they may already have been sent to the shipping company  .

Try these steps to resolve the 404 when printing:
- Open the order page and check that you’ve added and saved any required customs info; then download the label or forms from the order page .
- If you received a confirmation email, use the download link there to get the label and forms .
- Once downloaded, open the PDF in your preferred application and print the shipping label; print three copies of customs forms where requested .
If these steps don’t work, contact our Customer Support team for help with the download .

Read more in the Help Centre: Info about customs with smart shipping and How to resolve smart shipping customs issues.

expected_output:
A reserve price is a hidden minimum the seller is willing to accept for an object; it may only be set for objects with an estimated value of €200 or more and is agreed with our expert. You won’t see the exact reserve price before the auction ends, but the object page will show if a reserve price exists and the system will notify you if your bid doesn’t meet it.  

To find out the minimum price you’ll need to meet:
- Check the object page for a ‘reserve price’ indicator so you know one is set.
- Place a bid or set a max bid — if your max bid is higher than the reserve, the first bid will automatically match the reserve; if your bid is below it you’ll be informed in the bidding history. 
- Note that bidding can start from €1, 50% of the reserve or from the reserve itself, depending on the seller’s choice.

input: J’ai fais une offre mais c est écris offre trop basse mais ça me l a quand même validé. Qu es c e que ça veux dire ?
ai_suggestion: Il semble que votre offre ait été validée même si elle est considérée comme trop basse. Cela peut se produire si l'offre que vous avez faite est inférieure au prix de réserve fixé par le vendeur. Le prix de réserve est le montant minimum que le vendeur est prêt à accepter pour vendre l'objet. Si votre offre est inférieure à ce prix, elle ne sera pas acceptée comme une vente, mais elle peut toujours être enregistrée comme une offre valide dans le système.\n\nPour plus d'informations, vous pouvez consulter l'article [Pourquoi un enchérisseur peut-il enchérir en dessous du prix de réserve ?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/bidding-selling-process/why-can-a-bidder-bid-under-the-reserve-price).
gpt4_output: It appears your bid has been accepted even though it is considered too low. This can happen if your bid is lower than the reserve price set by the seller. The reserve price is the minimum amount the seller is willing to accept to sell the item. If your bid is lower than this price, it will not be accepted as a sale, but it may still be recorded as a valid bid in the system. For more information, you can consult the article [Why can a bidder bid below the reserve price?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/bidding-selling-process/why-can-a-bidder-bid-under-the-reserve-price).
expected_output:
When a message says your offer was ‘too low’ it usually means your bid or offer didn’t meet the seller’s reserve price or the platform’s minimum, so it wouldn’t automatically create a contract of sale at auction end. If the reserve isn’t met the object stays unsold, but sellers can still offer the object afterwards to the highest bidders — and the first bidder who accepts and pays can still take the object  .

What you can do next
- Check whether you used a max bid: if your max bid was below the reserve we place you at your limit (we don’t exceed your max) which can explain the ‘too low’ notice .  
- Look for an after‑auction offer email or message — for eligible objects offers may be sent to bidders and the first to accept and pay wins; payment rules depend on the object value (immediate payment or 72 hours) .  
- Review the object page and your inbox for specific payment instructions and act quickly if you want to secure the object.

Read more about offers to highest bidders in the Help Centre: How does offering my object to the highest bidders if the reserve price isn't met work?

input: Se un artista mi fa una proposta per acquistare una sua opera , io accetto  , ma poi contattandolo vedo che non è quello che cercavo , quindi non lo pago , cosa succede?
ai_suggestion: Se accetti una proposta per acquistare un'opera su Catawiki e poi decidi di non pagare, ci sono alcune conseguenze da considerare:\n\n- **Contratto di vendita**: Quando accetti un'offerta, entri in un contratto di vendita con il venditore. Sei quindi obbligato a pagare l'importo dell'acquisto entro 3 giorni dalla chiusura dell'asta o immediatamente se hai utilizzato l'opzione \
gpt4_output: If you accept an offer to buy an artwork on Catawiki and then decide not to pay, there are some consequences to consider: - **Sale Contract**: When you accept an offer, you enter into a sales contract with the seller. You are therefore obliged to pay the purchase amount within 3 days of the auction closing, or immediately if you used the option.
expected_output:
When you accept an offer to buy an object you enter into a contract of sale and are expected to pay — for accepted offers on objects under €2,500 buyers must pay immediately, and other purchases follow the payment deadlines in our Terms. If you don’t pay we’ll send payment reminders, may offer the object to other bidders and can cancel the sale; failure to pay can also lead to keeping the Buyer Protection fee and account measures  .

To avoid further consequences, do the following right away:
- Message the seller through our internal messaging to explain the situation and ask if they’ll agree to cancel the sale or accept an alternative solution — direct communication often helps resolve this.
- Be aware you may still be charged the Buyer Protection fee and could face account restrictions if you don’t fulfil the payment obligation.
- Keep records of all messages and any payment reminders you receive  .

That means once you accept, you agree to pay for the object and related costs. Buyers normally need to pay within three days, and if you do not pay, we will send payment reminders. If your order is cancelled because you did not pay in time, the seller may re-sell the object and you can still be charged the Buyer Protection fee.   

If you accepted but then realised it is not what you want, it is best to avoid simply not paying. Instead:

- Be aware that repeatedly cancelling orders can lead to restrictions on your ability to bid.

input: ce inseamna nu a fost atins pretul de rezerva?
ai_suggestion: Când un obiect nu atinge prețul de rezervă, înseamnă că oferta maximă făcută în timpul licitației nu a ajuns la prețul minim pe care vânzătorul este dispus să îl accepte pentru acel obiect. În acest caz, obiectul nu va fi vândut. Vânzătorii au opțiunea de a relista obiectul într-o nouă licitație, eventual ajustând prețul de rezervă pentru a crește șansele de vânzare.\n\nPentru mai multe detalii, poți consulta articolul [The reserve price for my object was not met, what are my options?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/unsold-lots/the-reserve-price-for-my-object-was-not-met-what-are-my-options).
gpt4_output: When an item does not meet its reserve price, it means that the highest bid made during the auction did not reach the minimum price that the seller is willing to accept for that item. In this case, the item will not be sold. Sellers have the option to relist the item in a new auction, possibly adjusting the reserve price to increase the chances of a sale.\n\nFor more details, you can consult the article [The reserve price for my object was not met, what are my options?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/unsold-lots/the-reserve-price-for-my-object-was-not-met-what-are-my-options).
expected_output:
A reserve price is a hidden minimum price a seller will accept for an object, set in consultation with the expert and normally available for objects with an estimated value of €200 or more . If the highest bid at the end of the auction is lower than that reserve price, the object will not be sold and no contract of sale is concluded .

Practical points to keep in mind:
- If you place a bid below the reserve price you’ll be notified and your bid will appear in the bidding history; automatic max bids will act so that, if your max bid exceeds the reserve, the first placed bid may match the reserve  .
- If the reserve isn’t met, sellers can resubmit with a lower or no reserve or may be offered the option to sell to the highest bidders if eligible .

[I’d like to place a bid on an object with a reserve price. What does this mean and how do I know what the reserve price is?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/reserve-prices/i-d-like-to-place-a-bid-on-an-object-with-a-reserve-price-what-does-this-mean-and-how-do-i-know-what-the-reserve-price-is) and [The reserve price for my object was not met, what are my options?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/unsold-lots/the-reserve-price-for-my-object-was-not-met-what-are-my-options). These Help Centre articles explain notification, bidding behaviour and seller options if the reserve isn’t met.

input: I have an open order that I need to cancel because I am not comfortable providing my personal information to the seller and they’re asking for my tax ID which I assume is my Social Security number and I am not comfortable giving that information to anyone. Please advise.
ai_suggestion:
If you're uncomfortable providing personal information like your tax ID or Social Security number to a seller, you have options to cancel your purchase under certain conditions:

- **Right of Withdrawal**: If you're an EU or UK citizen buying from a professional seller, you can cancel your purchase within 14 days of receiving the object without stating a reason. However, you will be responsible for the return shipping costs.

- **Professional Seller**: Ensure that the seller is a professional (indicated by a ‘PRO’ symbol on the object page) to use the right of withdrawal.

- **Contact the Seller**: If the seller is not professional or the right of withdrawal does not apply, you may need to negotiate directly with the seller for a cancellation.

For more detailed information, you can refer to the [I want to cancel my purchase](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-order-issues/i-want-to-cancel-my-purchase) page on Catawiki's help center.
gpt4_output:

- **Right of Withdrawal**: If you're an EU or UK citizen buying from a professional seller, you can cancel your purchase within 14 days of receiving the object without stating a reason. However, you will be responsible for the return shipping costs.

- **Professional Seller**: Ensure that the seller is a professional (indicated by a ‘PRO’ symbol on the object page) to use the right of withdrawal.

- **Contact the Seller**: If the seller is not professional or the right of withdrawal does not apply, you may need to negotiate directly with the seller for a cancellation.

For more detailed information, you can refer to the [I want to cancel my purchase](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-order-issues/i-want-to-cancel-my-purchase) page on Catawiki's help center.
expected_output:
As a buyer, your cancellation rights depend on who sold the object and where you live. If you bought from a professional seller and live in the EEA or the UK, you may cancel under the right of withdrawal within 14 days of delivery; otherwise bids and purchases are binding and cancellation is usually only possible for legitimate claims such as ‘not as described’ (we pause payment while investigating). 

Do this next:
- Do not share your Social Security number or tax ID by default; normally sellers only need your address and phone for shipping and, in some cases, an ID or phone for customs.    
- Ask the seller, using Catawiki’s internal messaging, to explain exactly why the tax ID is needed and whether a less sensitive alternative will suffice.   
- If you can’t agree, follow the cancellation and claims guidance in the Help Centre for next steps (for example if the object is ‘not as described’). 

Read the Help Centre article ‘I want to cancel my purchase’ for details, and review ‘How do I tell the difference between a Catawiki email and a phishing email’ to protect your data: [I want to cancel my purchase](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-order-issues/i-want-to-cancel-my-purchase) — [How do I tell the difference between a Catawiki email and a phishing email](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-terms/how-do-i-tell-the-difference-between-a-catawiki-email-and-a-phishing-email)

input: J'ai été paye sur payoneer mais l'argent n'a pas été transféré sur mon compte bancaire pourquoi
ai_suggestion:
Il peut y avoir plusieurs raisons pour lesquelles votre paiement via Payoneer n'a pas été transféré sur votre compte bancaire. Voici quelques points à vérifier :

- **Détails bancaires incorrects** : Assurez-vous que les détails de votre compte bancaire sont corrects et complets dans votre compte Payoneer. Si vous recevez vos paiements via Payoneer, vous devez effectuer les ajustements de paiement directement sur votre compte Payoneer.

- **Vérification de l'identité** : Il est possible que votre identité doive être vérifiée par notre partenaire de paiement. Assurez-vous que toutes les informations nécessaires sont fournies et vérifiées.

- **Fréquence de paiement** : Vérifiez les paramètres de fréquence de paiement dans votre compte. Par défaut, la fréquence de paiement est définie sur quotidienne, mais vous pouvez la modifier selon vos préférences.

Pour plus d'informations, vous pouvez consulter l'article [Comment modifier mes coordonnées bancaires](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/payment-settings/how-do-i-change-my-bank-details).
gpt4_output: There could be several reasons why your payment via Payoneer hasn't been transferred to your bank account. Here are a few things to check: - **Incorrect bank details**: Make sure your bank account details are correct and complete in your Payoneer account. If you receive your payments via Payoneer, you need to make payment adjustments directly in your Payoneer account. - **Identity verification**: Your identity may need to be verified by our payment partner. Make sure all the necessary information is provided and verified. - **Payment frequency**: Check the payment frequency settings in your account. By default, the payment frequency is set to daily, but you can change it to suit your preferences. For more information, you can refer to the article [How to change my bank details](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/payment-settings/how-do-i-change-my-bank-details).
expected_output:
If you were paid via Payoneer but the funds haven’t reached your bank account, it’s usually due to the payment provider’s checks or your payout setup. Payoneer (and Stripe) can ask for additional documentation or identity verification before releasing payouts, and payouts sent via Payoneer must be managed in your Payoneer account. Payments can also be paused for reasons such as an open claim, incorrect or missing bank details, or an incomplete payment setup   .

To resolve this, check the following:
- Verify your Payoneer account status and any requests for documents on Payoneer (update bank details there if needed) .
- Confirm your payment settings and payment frequency in your Catawiki Settings and that you completed any identity checks .
- Make sure the sale’s shipping status and tracking are correct, and look for any emails about an open claim that would pause payment .
- If everything looks correct to you, contact Payoneer for the transfer status.

Read more in the Help Centre: ‘Why haven’t I been paid yet?’ and ‘How do I change my bank details?’.
user_question: I was paid via Payoneer, but the money hasn't been transferred to my bank account. Why?

input: Hoe verander ik mijn adresgegevens
ai_suggestion:
Je kunt je adresgegevens eenvoudig wijzigen via je accountinstellingen op Catawiki. Hier zijn de stappen die je moet volgen:

- Log in op je Catawiki-account.
- Ga naar 'Instellingen'.
- Zoek het gedeelte 'Adressen'.
- Bewerk je adres en sla de wijzigingen op.

Zorg ervoor dat het adres een fysiek adres is en geen postbus. Het moet een straatnaam, nummer, postcode en plaats bevatten. Als je je land wilt wijzigen en je account gebruikt om te verkopen, moet je een nieuw account aanmaken met de bijgewerkte land- en adresgegevens.

Voor meer informatie, bekijk ons artikel over [hoe je je persoonlijke gegevens kunt bijwerken](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-account-settings/where-can-i-adjust-my-personal-information).
gpt4_output: You can easily change your address details through your account settings on Catawiki. Here are the steps to follow: - Log in to your Catawiki account. - Go to 'Settings'. - Find the 'Addresses' section. - Edit your address and save the changes. - Make sure the address is a physical street address and not a PO Box. It must include a street name, number, postal code, and city. If you want to change your country and use your account to sell, you will need to create a new account with the updated country and address details. For more information, please see our article on [how to update your personal information](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-account-settings/where-can-i-adjust-my-personal-information).
expected_output:
You can update your address from your account Settings. Sign in, go to Settings and open the ‘Addresses’ section to edit your shipping or billing address, then save your edits. The shipping address must be a physical address (no PO Box) and include street name, number, postal code and town. If you’ve registered as a seller or started seller registration, you won’t be able to change your country and may need to create a new account instead .

Actionable guidance:
- Sign in and open Settings → Addresses, then select ‘Change’ to edit your shipping address.
- To add a billing address, choose ‘I am using a different billing address’ at the bottom of the Addresses page.
- Double‑check the details are accurate and complete – our payment partners or sellers may ask for verification.
- Changing certain details (email, phone, payment info, or if your account has been inactive for over a year) can require a Secure Code we send to your email or phone .

For step‑by‑step instructions and screenshots, read the Help Centre article: [How can I update my personal data?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-account-settings/where-can-i-adjust-my-personal-information) It also explains rules about changing country and billing address options.
user_question: How do I change my address details?

Catawiki doesn’t offer a dedicated ‘salesperson’ phone line; instead we ask you to contact us through the Help Centre so we can direct your request to the right team. Sign in first so we can give personalised help about specific objects or selling queries. The Help Centre’s contact flow asks you to choose a reason and then shows the most suitable contact method — live chat, email or phone for certain cases — so you reach the right people quickly  .

To speak with someone about sales or selling opportunities:
- Sign in to your Catawiki account to speed up the process.  
- Open the relevant Help Centre article for your question and use the ‘Contact us’ button at the bottom.  
- Select the contact reason and provide details so we can route you correctly; you’ll then be offered live chat, email or phone where available. Support is offered in multiple languages depending on the method .

Read more and start the contact process in the Help Centre: [I’m a buyer. How do I contact Catawiki’s customer support?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/most-asked-questions-of-buyers/i-m-a-buyer-how-do-i-contact-catawiki-s-customer-support)

ai_suggestion:
Para vender en Catawiki, sigue estos pasos:

- **Regístrate como vendedor**: Crea una cuenta gratuita en Catawiki.
- **Envía tus objetos**: El proceso de envío te guiará paso a paso, desde subir buenas imágenes de tu objeto hasta proporcionar la información correcta que ayude a los compradores a encontrar y confiar en tu objeto.
- **Confirmación de identidad**: Deberás confirmar tu identidad con nuestro socio de pagos (Stripe o Payoneer) para garantizar la seguridad del proceso de pujas.
- **Evaluación por expertos**: Un experto revisará tu envío para asegurarse de que tu objeto tenga todos los detalles necesarios para atraer a los compradores.
- **Durante la subasta**: Las pujas comienzan desde 1 €, al 50% del precio de reserva, o al precio de reserva, dependiendo de la configuración que elijas.
- **Envío y pagos**: Te notificaremos una vez que el comprador haya transferido su pago. Luego, solo queda enviar tus objetos o prepararlos para su recogida.

Para más detalles, puedes consultar el artículo [Cómo funciona la venta en Catawiki](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/become-a-seller/how-does-selling-on-catawiki-work).
gpt4_output:
To sell on Catawiki, follow these steps:

- **Register as a seller**: Create a free account on Catawiki.
- **Submit your items**: The submission process will guide you step by step, from uploading good images of your item to providing the correct information to help buyers find and trust your item.
- **Identity confirmation**: You will need to confirm your identity with our payment partner (Stripe or Payoneer) to ensure the security of the bidding process.
- **Expert evaluation**: An expert will review your submission to ensure your item has all the necessary details to attract buyers.
- **During the auction**: Bidding starts from €1, up to 50% of the reserve price, or at the reserve price, depending on the settings you choose.
- **Shipping and payments**: We will notify you once the buyer has transferred their payment. Then, all that remains is to send your items or prepare them for collection.

For more details, you can consult the article [How selling on Catawiki works](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/become-a-seller/how-does-selling-on-catawiki-work).
expected_output:
You start selling by choosing Sell and then Start selling on our website; the submission flow guides you step‑by‑step and an in‑house expert reviews your object before it’s scheduled for auction . You can upload photos and complete submissions from a mobile phone or tablet — use your device’s camera or add images from your gallery, and the submission tool can show a QR code to continue on another device .

Try this practical approach:
- Open Catawiki in your mobile browser and choose Sell → Start selling to begin a submission .  
- Add clear photos, title, description, estimated value and shipping info from your device .  
- Complete the one‑time identity verification with our payment partner when prompted so you can receive payouts .  
- Preview and submit; an expert will contact you if they need more information .

Read the Help Centre articles [How do I register as a seller?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/become-a-seller/how-do-i-register-as-a-seller) and [How does the submission process work?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/submitting-lots/how-does-the-submission-process-work) for step‑by‑step instructions.

input: Ik doe melding van nagemaakte objecten, incl. voorwerpen die inbreuk maken op intellectuele eigendomsrechten van Harley-Davidson zijnde de kavels met nummer: 99163671, 99245340, 99245279, 99359453, 99328005, 99163733, 99315969, 99222484, 99321550, 99352035, 99251106, 99291234, 99179984, 99388514. Al deze aangeboden artikelen zijn zonder toestemming, en of licentie van Harley Davidson gefabriceerd. Dit is te zien aan het ontbreken van Trademark ™ teken en ontbreken van verpakking met fabrikant.
ai_suggestion: Het is belangrijk om nagemaakte objecten of objecten die inbreuk maken op intellectuele eigendomsrechten te melden bij Catawiki. We hebben een [Misuse Policy](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-terms/misuse-policy) die ons helpt om een veilige en betrouwbare marktplaats te behouden. Als een kunstenaar, merk of rechthebbende meldt dat hun werk zonder toestemming wordt aangeboden, onderzoeken we de claim. Als de claim geldig is, verwijderen we het object en kunnen we een waarschuwing geven.\n\nOm een object te melden dat volgens jou niet op Catawiki thuishoort, kun je gebruik maken van de ‘Contact Us’ knop of een e-mail sturen naar dsa [at] catawiki.com. Zorg ervoor dat je zoveel mogelijk details verstrekt over het object en waarom je denkt dat het onze beleidsregels schendt.
gpt4_output: It is important to report counterfeit items or items that infringe intellectual property rights to Catawiki. We have a Misuse Policy that helps us maintain a safe and reliable marketplace. If an artist, brand, or rights holder reports that their work is being offered for sale without permission, we will investigate the claim. If the claim is valid, we will remove the item and may issue a warning. To report an item that you believe does not belong on Catawiki, you can use the ‘Contact Us’ button or send an email to dsa [at] catawiki.com. Please be sure to provide as much detail as possible about the item and why you think it violates our policies.

All of these offered items have been manufactured without permission and/or license from Harley Davidson. This is evident from the lack of a Trademark ™ sign and the lack of packaging with manufacturer.

input: Bonjour, je suis le vendeur du lot “Vase Saint‑Louis ‘Opéra’ – Cristal Tirage Limité – Art Déco – Création 2000 – Pièce Unique” (réserve 2 700 €). Je demande la modification immédiate du titre et du descriptif avec mon texte final, et la programmation de la fin de l’enchère dimanche 7 décembre à 20h. Merci de confirmer rapidement la procédure pour appliquer ces changements.
ai_suggestion:
Pour modifier le titre et la description de votre lot, ainsi que pour programmer la fin de l'enchère, voici ce que vous devez savoir :

- **Modification du titre et de la description** : Vous pouvez ajuster votre annonce avant qu'elle ne soit sélectionnée pour la vente par notre expert. Une fois que votre objet est en vente, il n'est plus possible de modifier le titre ou la description sans l'approbation de l'expert.

- **Programmation de la fin de l'enchère** : Les lots approuvés sont programmés pour une vente aux enchères dès que possible. Vous recevrez un message automatisé dès que votre lot sera programmé pour une vente aux enchères.

Pour toute modification après l'approbation de l'expert, il est nécessaire de contacter directement notre équipe pour discuter des changements possibles.

Pour plus d'informations, vous pouvez consulter l'article [My lot has been approved for auction. When will it be scheduled for auction?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/lot-status-auction-planning/my-lot-has-been-approved-for-auction-when-will-it-be-scheduled-for-auction).
gpt4_output:
To change the title and description of your lot, and to schedule the end of the auction, here's what you need to know:

- **Changing the title and description:** You can adjust your listing before it is selected for sale by our expert. Once your item is listed for sale, it is no longer possible to change the title or description without the expert's approval.

- **Scheduling the end of the auction:** Approved lots are scheduled for auction as soon as possible. You will receive an automated message as soon as your lot is scheduled for auction.

For any changes after expert approval, you must contact our team directly to discuss the possible changes.

For more information, you can refer to the article [My lot has been approved for auction.] When will it be scheduled for auction?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/lot-status-auction-planning/my-lot-has-been-approved-for-auction-when-will-it-be-scheduled-for-auction).
expected_output: transactional-question
user_question: Hello, I am the seller of the lot “Saint-Louis ‘Opéra’ Vase – Limited Edition Crystal – Art Deco – Created in 2000 – Unique Piece” (reserve price €2700). I request the immediate modification of the title and description with my final text, and that the auction be scheduled to end on Sunday, December 7th at 8 PM. Please confirm the procedure for implementing these changes as soon as possible.

gpt4_output: When an auction on Catawiki ends with the status \
expected_output:
When an object shows ‘no longer available’ at the end of an auction it means the object can no longer be bought or bid on. This most commonly means the auction ended and the highest bidder is obliged to pay for the object . It can also mean someone used buy now and the object was sold immediately , or that the object was removed from the auction .

Check what to do next:
- Look at the object page for the final status and any messages from us .
- Check your email for a payment or sale confirmation if you were the highest bidder or used buy now .
- If the reserve price wasn’t met, the object may be unsold; we may offer after‑auction options to the seller and bidders .
- If the object was removed, see our removal policy for reasons and how to appeal .

Read more in the Help centre: [What do the different lot statuses mean?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/lot-status-auction-planning/what-do-the-different-lot-statuses-mean) and [Can Catawiki remove my object from auction?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/during-auction-changes-to-removal-of-lots/can-catawiki-remove-my-object-from-auction)

input: Come posso ritirare un oggetto se non raggiunge il prezzo che voglio
ai_suggestion:
Se desideri ritirare un oggetto perché non ha raggiunto il prezzo desiderato, è importante sapere che una volta che l'oggetto è stato accettato e pianificato per la vendita, non puoi ritirarlo a meno che non ricevi un'approvazione esplicita da parte nostra. Se approviamo la tua richiesta, puoi ritirare l'oggetto fino a un giorno prima che vada all'asta. Una volta che l'oggetto è in vendita, non è più possibile ritirarlo.

Ecco alcuni punti chiave da considerare:

- **Ritiro prima dell'asta**: Puoi ritirare l'oggetto solo con approvazione esplicita e fino a un giorno prima dell'inizio dell'asta.
- **Ritiro durante l'asta**: Non è possibile ritirare l'oggetto una volta che l'asta è iniziata.
- **Conseguenze**: Se ritiri un oggetto dopo che la vendita è iniziata, potresti essere responsabile per eventuali danni e potresti incorrere in una penalità.

Per ulteriori dettagli, puoi consultare l'articolo [Can I edit my reserve price?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/lot-submission-changes-to-removal-of-lots/can-i-edit-my-reserve-price) per informazioni su come gestire il prezzo di riserva.
gpt4_output:
If you wish to withdraw an item because it hasn't reached your desired price, it's important to know that once the item has been accepted and scheduled for sale, you cannot withdraw it unless we explicitly approve it. If we approve your request, you can withdraw the item up to one day before it goes to auction. Once an item is listed for sale, it can no longer be collected.

Here are some key points to consider:

- **Collection before the auction**: You can only collect your item with explicit approval and up to one day before the auction starts.
- **Collection during the auction**: You cannot collect your item once the auction has started.
- **Consequences**: If you collect an item after the sale has started, you may be liable for any damages and may incur a penalty.

For more details, you can consult the article [Can I edit my reserve price?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/lot-submission-changes-to-removal-of-lots/can-i-edit-my-reserve-price) for information on how to manage your reserve price.
expected_output:
Once you’ve submitted an object and it’s been accepted and scheduled for sale, you can’t withdraw it after the listing is live. We may approve withdrawal only up to one day before the auction starts and only with explicit permission; once the auction has started withdrawal is no longer possible. If you set a reserve price, the lot will not be sold if bids don’t meet that reserve.  

Your practical options are:
- Resubmit the object for another auction and lower or remove the reserve price to increase the chances of selling. 
- If eligible, accept an email invitation to offer the object to the highest bidders for the top bid — eligibility and offer processes depend on the object value (different procedures for under €2,500, €2,500–€5,000 and over €5,000). 
- Bear in mind that withdrawing after the auction starts can create liability and penalties, so resubmission or offering to the highest bidders are the usual routes. 

Read more in the Help Centre: [The reserve price for my object was not met, what are my options?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/unsold-lots/the-reserve-price-for-my-object-was-not-met-what-are-my-options) and [I feel the highest bid is too low. Am I required to sell my lot?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/bidding-selling-process/i-feel-the-highest-bid-is-too-low-am-i-required-to-sell-my-lot).

input: Ho purtroppo visti dopo il presso della spedizione, è possibile revocare l'offerta?
ai_suggestion: Le offerte su Catawiki sono vincolanti, il che significa che non puoi revocare un'offerta una volta fatta. Tuttavia, in alcuni casi eccezionali, come un errore tecnico o un errore di digitazione, potremmo revocare un'offerta accettata in precedenza.\n\nSe hai bisogno di ulteriori informazioni su come funziona il processo di offerta, puoi consultare il nostro articolo [Buyer Terms of Use](https://www.catawiki.com/en/pages/p/buyer-terms).
gpt4_output: Bids on Catawiki are binding, which means you cannot revoke a bid once placed. However, in some exceptional cases, such as a technical error or typo, we may revoke a previously accepted bid.\n\nIf you need more information about how the bidding process works, you can consult our [Buyer Terms of Use](https://www.catawiki.com/en/pages/p/buyer-terms).
expected_output:
Unfortunately, you can’t cancel or withdraw a bid or offer once it’s placed on Catawiki — all bids and uses of buy now are binding and cannot be retracted unless outbid. If you’ve used buy now or the seller accepted your offer, that forms a contract of sale and you’re required to pay the purchase price and any shipping costs.  

Try the following:
- Message the seller via Catawiki’s internal messaging to explain that you only saw the shipping cost afterwards and ask if they’ll agree to cancel or adjust the shipping — cancellations normally need the seller’s agreement. 
- If the sale is already completed and you live in the EEA or the UK and bought from a professional seller, you may have a 14‑day right of withdrawal after delivery; inform the seller within that period. 
- If the object isn’t as described, report it promptly so we can investigate. 

For full details, read the Help Centre articles ‘I wish to withdraw or cancel an earlier bid’ and ‘I want to cancel my purchase’. [I wish to withdraw or cancel an earlier bid](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/bidding-issues/i-wish-to-withdraw-or-cancel-an-earlier-bid) [I want to cancel my purchase](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-order-issues/i-want-to-cancel-my-purchase)

input: Como puedo entrar en nuestra página para vender y comprar
ai_suggestion:
Para comenzar a vender y comprar en Catawiki, sigue estos pasos:

### Registro
- **Crear una cuenta**: Haz clic en el botón \
gpt4_output:
To start buying and selling on Catawiki, follow these steps:

### Registration
- **Create an account**: Click on the button \
expected_output:
To buy or sell on Catawiki you can browse the marketplace without an account, but you need a Catawiki account to place bids, use Buy now, or submit objects for sale. To register you’ll need an email address and a secure password and you’ll receive a confirmation email to activate your account . To register as a seller, select ‘Sell’ then ‘Start selling’ and complete the required identity checks (email and SMS) before submitting objects .

Follow these steps to get started:
- To buy: create a free account, sign in, then bid or use Buy now on the object page; pay within three days after winning (Buy now requires immediate payment) .
- To sell: choose ‘Sell’ → ‘Start selling’, state whether you’re a private or professional seller, complete ID verification, upload high‑quality photos and detailed object information; our experts will review and schedule your object for auction .

For step‑by‑step instructions, read these Help Centre articles: [How do I register?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-registering/how-do-i-register) and [How do I register as a seller?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/become-a-seller/how-do-i-register-as-a-seller)

input: Nasıl üyeliği iptal ederim
ai_suggestion:
Üyeliğinizi iptal etmek için aşağıdaki adımları izleyebilirsiniz:

- Son işlem tarihinizden itibaren 60 gün beklemeniz gerekmektedir. Bu süre, açık artırmalardaki tekliflerinizi tamamlamanız ve ödenmemiş faturalarınızı kapatmanız için gereklidir.
- Masaüstü tarayıcınızdan Ayarlar sayfanıza gidin.
- Sol alt köşede bulunan ‘Hesabımı sil’ seçeneğini seçin.
- Bu işlemi yaptıktan sonra, hesabınız 2 hafta içinde kalıcı olarak silinecektir.

Hesabınızı sildikten sonra, tekrar üye olsanız bile geçmiş işlemlerinizi göremezsiniz. Daha fazla bilgi için [Hesabımı nasıl silerim?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-account-settings/how-do-i-delete-my-account) sayfasını ziyaret edebilirsiniz.
gpt4_output:
To cancel your membership, you can follow the steps below:

- You must wait 60 days from your last transaction date. This period is required for you to complete your bids on auctions and close any outstanding invoices.
- Go to your Settings page on your desktop browser.
- Select 'Delete my account' in the bottom left corner.
- After doing this, your account will be permanently deleted within 2 weeks.

After deleting your account, you will not be able to see your past transactions, even if you sign up again. For more information, please visit [How do I delete my account?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-account-settings/how-do-i-delete-my-account).
expected_output:
To cancel your membership you need to delete your Catawiki account. Before you can do this you must wait 60 days after your last transaction (buying or selling) and ensure there are no active bids, unpaid invoices, sold objects awaiting payment, objects you’re selling in active auctions, or open claims with our Customer Support team. Deleting is done from your Settings page and the account is permanently deleted within two weeks.  

To proceed, follow these steps:
- Sign in and open your Settings page.  
- Select ‘Delete my account’ at the bottom left and follow the on‑screen instructions to confirm.  
- Before deleting, close or resolve any active bids, pay outstanding invoices, remove objects you’re selling, and resolve open claims.  
- If your account is suspended or has recent transactions you may be unable to delete it; we’ll notify you on screen if that applies. 

Read more in the Help Centre for full details and exceptions: [How do I delete my account?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-account-settings/how-do-i-delete-my-account).

input:
Vi scrivo riguardo la vendita dei whisky glenfiddich e altri che sono in transito in Germania..perché non succede niente e non ho notizie del mio pacco che ho spedito?
Grazie per l'attenzione
ai_suggestion:
Sembra che il tuo pacco possa essere in ritardo a causa del passaggio attraverso la dogana. Ecco alcuni punti da considerare:

- **Ritardi doganali**: Gli ordini che passano attraverso la dogana tendono a richiedere più tempo. Se sono passati 5 giorni dalla data di consegna stimata e il tuo ordine non è stato consegnato, ti invieremo un'email o una notifica.

- **Controllo dello stato di spedizione**: Puoi controllare lo stato del tuo ordine sulla pagina dell'ordine o sul sito web della compagnia di spedizioni utilizzando il codice di tracciamento.

- **Azioni da intraprendere**: Se il pacco non è stato consegnato entro 5 giorni dalla data stimata, è importante farcelo sapere in modo da poter sospendere il pagamento al venditore e indagare.

Per ulteriori dettagli, puoi consultare l'articolo [I've paid for my object. Why haven't I received it yet?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/most-asked-questions-of-buyers/i-ve-paid-for-my-object-why-haven-t-i-received-it-yet).
gpt4_output:
It looks like your package may be delayed due to customs clearance. Here are some things to consider:

- **Customs Delays**: Orders clearing through customs tend to take longer. If 5 days have passed since the estimated delivery date and your order hasn't been delivered, we'll send you an email or notification.

- **Checking Shipping Status**: You can check the status of your order on the order page or on the shipping company's website using the tracking code.

- **Actions to Take**: If your package hasn't been delivered within 5 days of the estimated delivery date, it's important to let us know so we can hold payment to the seller and investigate.

For more details, please see the article [I've paid for my item. Why haven't I received it yet?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/most-asked-questions-of-buyers/i-ve-paid-for-my-object-why-haven-t-i-received-it-yet).
expected_output: transactional-question

I'm writing to you regarding the sale of Glenfiddich whiskies and others that are in transit in Germany. Why hasn't anything happened and I haven't heard from my package that I shipped?
Thank you for your attention.

input:
Hello, 
I hope you are well. 
Several of my recently submitted artworks have been in "Submitted" status for a few days and have not yet been reviewed. 
Could you please check if everything is okay with my listings?

Thank you in advance.
Kind regards,
Aziz
ai_suggestion: It seems like your artworks are still in the \
gpt4_output: It seems like your artworks are still in the \
expected_output:
When an object shows the Submitted status it means you’ve forwarded the listing to one of our experts for evaluation; they assess suitability, condition and presentation before approving it for auction. Our experts aim to complete reviews within a week, but timing can vary by category and for themed or especially busy auctions it can take a little longer. You’ll receive a notification once an expert has approved the object.  

What you can do now:
- Check your Submissions page to monitor progress — newly submitted objects appear there.   
- If the submission hasn’t been reviewed yet you can edit it via the actions dropdown.   
- Add a message to the expert at the bottom of your submission; the expert will reply as part of the review (typically within one working day to one week).   
- If the review still hasn’t happened after a reasonable period, contact our Customer Support team for assistance. 

Read more in the Help Centre: I've submitted a lot for auction — when will it be reviewed? and What do the different lot statuses mean?

Could you please check if everything is okay with my listings?

Thank you in advance.
Kind regards,
Aziz

expected_output:
Vouchers on Catawiki are usually linked to your account and will appear automatically at checkout; when you select a voucher we’ll deduct its value from the order total so you can pay the reduced amount. If a voucher isn’t linked to your account you can choose ‘Add voucher’ at checkout and enter the code — we’ll then apply the discount and show the reduced total in the summary .

To use your voucher:
- Sign in and go to checkout; select the voucher shown or choose ‘Add voucher’ and enter the code exactly (including any dashes or special characters).
- Check the checkout summary to confirm the discount has been applied.
- Make sure the voucher conditions are met (expiry date, any minimum final bid — the final bid excludes shipping costs and the Buyer Protection fee).
- Remember each voucher is single use and only one voucher can be used per order .

Read more in the Help centre: [How do I use my voucher?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/vouchers/how-do-i-activate-my-voucher-code) or [Why isn’t my voucher working?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/vouchers/i-can-t-use-my-voucher)

input: Wann bezahlen
ai_suggestion: Zahlungen bei Catawiki müssen innerhalb von 3 Tagen nach Ende der Auktion erfolgen. Wenn Sie die Option \
gpt4_output: Payments at Catawiki must be made within 3 days of the auction's end. If you select the option \
expected_output:
You must pay for an object you’ve won within three days of the auction ending, or immediately if you used Buy now — this covers the purchase price plus the Buyer Protection fee and any shipping or import costs. Once payment is received, our payment partner holds the funds in escrow and we notify the seller so they can ship or prepare the package for pickup .  

To complete payment and avoid penalties, follow these steps:  
- Use the payment link in the congratulation email or go to your Orders page under ‘Unpaid’ to pay; a variety of online methods and cards are supported .  
- After you’ve paid, you can message the seller to arrange shipping or pickup; sellers must ship within three working days of confirmed payment .  
- If you miss the three‑day deadline, we’ll send reminders and may offer the object to other bidders or cancel the sale; you may still be charged the Buyer Protection fee .  

Read more in the Help centre: [I have won a lot. How do I pay?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/payment-options/i-have-won-a-lot-how-do-i-pay)

input: Is it normal that cost to Latvia costs more then item i buy?
ai_suggestion:
It's not uncommon for shipping costs to sometimes exceed the price of the object you purchase, especially when shipping internationally. Here are a few points to consider:

- **Shipping Costs**: The shipping cost, delivery time, and insurance can vary depending on the shipping company selected by the seller. Sellers are required to accurately calculate their shipping costs, but sometimes the actual costs can be higher or lower than estimated.

- **Excessive Shipping Costs**: If you find that you've overpaid for shipping fees once your object arrives, you should contact the seller via your order detail page. The seller is required to reimburse you directly for any excess shipping costs.

- **International Shipping**: Shipping costs can be higher for international deliveries due to additional factors like customs duties and clearing charges.

For more detailed information, you can refer to the [Catawiki Help Center](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-shipping-costs/the-shipping-costs-seem-excessively-high-what-do-i-do).

expected_output:
It can be normal for the shipping cost to be higher than the object price, especially for international or secure shipping. As the buyer you’re responsible for the shipping costs shown on the object page, and those costs can include the shipping company fee, insurance, packing and handling, and any customs or import charges. Sellers are required to set shipping costs when they list an object, so check the shipping details before you bid or buy   .

Practical steps you can take:
- Check the object’s shipping costs and estimated delivery on the object page before bidding.   
- If the shipping feels excessive after delivery, message the seller from your order page and ask for a breakdown or reimbursement; sellers are required to reimburse any excess shipping you paid.   
- Consider pickup, combined shipping or asking the seller about smarter shipping options to reduce costs. 

Read more in the Help centre: Who pays for the shipping costs? and The shipping costs seem excessively high. What do I do?

input: Non riesco piu a fare un offerta..... Cosa devo fare???
ai_suggestion:
Se hai difficoltà a fare un'offerta su Catawiki, ci sono alcune cose che puoi controllare:

- **Verifica il tuo account**: Assicurati che il tuo account sia attivo e che non ci siano restrizioni che ti impediscono di fare offerte.
- **Controlla i dettagli di pagamento**: Assicurati che i dettagli di pagamento siano aggiornati e corretti. Potrebbe essere necessario aggiornare le informazioni di pagamento nel tuo account.
- **Problemi tecnici**: A volte, problemi tecnici possono impedire di fare offerte. Prova a ricaricare la pagina o a utilizzare un altro browser.
- **Limiti di offerta**: Se stai cercando di fare un'offerta su un oggetto con un prezzo di riserva, assicurati che la tua offerta sia al di sopra del prezzo di riserva.

Per ulteriori dettagli, puoi consultare la [pagina di aiuto di Catawiki](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/bidding-selling-process/why-can-a-bidder-bid-under-the-reserve-price).
gpt4_output:
If you're having trouble bidding on Catawiki, there are a few things you can check:

- **Verify your account**: Make sure your account is active and that there are no restrictions preventing you from bidding.
- **Check your payment details**: Make sure your payment details are up to date and correct. You may need to update your payment information in your account.
- **Technical issues**: Sometimes, technical issues can prevent you from bidding. Try reloading the page or using a different browser.
- **Bidding limits**: If you're trying to bid on an item with a reserve price, make sure your bid is above the reserve price.

For more details, you can consult the Catawiki help page (https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/bidding-selling-process/why-can-a-bidder-bid-under-the-reserve-price).
expected_output:
It can happen for several reasons. All bids are binding on Catawiki, and if you’ve previously failed to pay for a won object we may block you from placing further bids, which would prevent you making offers . We may also suspend or limit accounts for breaches of our Terms, and auctions or ‘buy now’ options can be unavailable if the auction has ended or the buy now window has closed  . Make sure your account is activated and signed in correctly .

Try the following checks to resolve it quickly:
- Sign in and confirm your account is activated; follow the verification steps if required .  
- Check for any unpaid orders or unpaid won objects and pay them — unpaid orders can lead to bidding blocks .  
- Open the object page to see whether the auction has ended or buy now is no longer available .  
- Look for any emails from us explaining a suspension or restriction on your account .

Read more in the Help centre: [I wish to withdraw or cancel an earlier bid](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/bidding-issues/i-wish-to-withdraw-or-cancel-an-earlier-bid) and [I can't sign in. What should I do?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/most-asked-questions-of-buyers/i-cant-sign-in-what-should-i-do).

input: Ho fatto delle offerte per lotto di pepite d'oro n. 98405607- e n. 98412687. Nella descrizione parla di pepite d'oro 'ciascuna' di 0.5 g di peso. Poi nel dettaglio parla invece di peso tot di 0.5. Avendo il dubbio sono andato a vedere le recensioni dei vari acquirenti e vedo che qualcuno si è lamentato. Come posso sapere se effettivamente i lotti sono per un peso di 0.5 g a pepita x le pepite citate oppure si tratta di un lotto di più pepite per un totale peso di 0.5?
ai_suggestion: Non sono riuscito a trovare informazioni specifiche sui lotti di pepite d'oro che hai menzionato. Ti consiglio di controllare attentamente la descrizione del lotto sulla pagina dell'asta per chiarimenti. Se la descrizione non è chiara, potrebbe essere utile contattare il venditore direttamente tramite la piattaforma per ottenere ulteriori dettagli sul peso delle pepite.  Per ulteriori informazioni su come gestire le descrizioni degli oggetti, puoi consultare le [linee guida per la presentazione degli oggetti](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/specific-guidelines-interiors-decorations/silver-gold-decor-submission-guidelines) su Catawiki.
gpt4_output: I couldn't find specific information about the gold nugget lots you mentioned. I recommend carefully reviewing the lot description on the auction page for clarification. If the description isn't clear, it may be helpful to contact the seller directly through the platform for more details on the nugget weight. For more information on how to manage item descriptions, you can consult Catawiki's [item submission guidelines](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/specific-guidelines-interiors-decorations/silver-gold-decor-submission-guidelines).

user_question: I placed bids on gold nugget lots, No. 98405607 and No. 98412687. The description states that each nugget weighs 0.5 g. The details state that the total weight is 0.5 g. How can I tell if the lots actually weigh 0.5 g per nugget for the nuggets mentioned, or if it's a single lot of nuggets for a total weight of 0.5 g?

input: J'aimerais savoir lorsqu'il est indiqué "placez l'offre maximale", quel est ce prix?
ai_suggestion: Lorsque vous placez une "offre maximale" sur Catawiki, cela signifie que vous définissez le montant maximum que vous êtes prêt à offrir pour un objet. Voici comment cela fonctionne :  - **Offre automatique** : Vous entrez le montant maximum que vous êtes prêt à payer, et Catawiki placera automatiquement les enchères pour vous, en utilisant les plus petits incréments possibles pour vous maintenir en tête. - **Contrôle total** : Vous ne dépasserez jamais le montant maximum que vous avez défini. Si les enchères dépassent votre offre maximale, vous serez informé afin que vous puissiez décider si vous souhaitez enchérir à nouveau.  Pour plus de détails, vous pouvez consulter l'article [Qu'est-ce qu'une offre maximale et comment fonctionne-t-elle ?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/max-bids/what-is-a-max-bid-and-how-does-it-work).
gpt4_output: When you place a "maximum bid" on Catawiki, it means you're setting the maximum amount you're willing to pay for an item. Here's how it works: - **Automatic Bidding**: You enter the maximum amount you're willing to pay, and Catawiki will automatically place bids for you, using the smallest possible increments to keep you in the lead. - **Full Control**: You'll never exceed the maximum amount you've set. If the bids exceed your maximum bid, you'll be notified so you can decide if you want to bid again. For more details, you can consult the article [What is a maximum bid and how does it work?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/max-bids/what-is-a-max-bid-and-how-does-it-work).
expected_output:
When you choose ‘place the maximum bid’ you set the highest amount you’re willing to pay and we’ll bid on your behalf up to that limit. We automatically place the next minimum bid for you (the smallest increment needed to become the highest bidder) and we will never go above the max bid you set . In some situations—such as a reserve price or competing auto bids—your bid may immediately reach your maximum amount .

Practical points to keep in mind:
- We place the next minimum bid according to the current highest bid and the bidding increments shown in our increments table (so the exact amount depends on the current bid) .  
- If a reserve price equals or exceeds your limit, we may place a bid up to your max to try to meet the reserve .  
- You can increase your max bid by placing a new, higher max bid, but you can’t lower or cancel a bid once it’s set .

Read more in the Help Centre: [What is a max bid and how does it work?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/max-bids/what-is-a-max-bid-and-how-does-it-work) and [What kinds of bids can I place on Catawiki?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/bidding-basics/what-kinds-of-bids-can-i-place-on-catawiki)

input: O que é o preço de reserva
ai_suggestion: O preço de reserva é o preço mínimo que um vendedor está disposto a aceitar por um objeto. Se o seu lance for o mais alto, mas ainda assim inferior ao preço de reserva definido pelo vendedor, você não entrará em um contrato de venda com o vendedor.  Para mais informações, você pode visitar o artigo [O que acontece se um lance máximo for colocado em um lote com um preço de reserva?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/max-bids/what-happens-if-a-max-bid-is-placed-on-a-lot-with-a-reserve-price).
gpt4_output: The reserve price is the minimum price a seller is willing to accept for an item. If your bid is the highest, but still below the reserve price set by the seller, you will not enter into a sales contract with the seller. For more information, you can visit the article [What happens if a maximum bid is placed on a lot with a reserve price?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/max-bids/what-happens-if-a-max-bid-is-placed-on-a-lot-with-a-reserve-price).
expected_output:
A reserve price is the hidden minimum price a seller is prepared to accept for an object; it’s set in consultation with our expert and may only be used for objects with an expected revenue of at least €200. If the reserve price isn’t met when the auction ends, the object will not be sold and no contract of sale is concluded  .

Practical points to help you bid or sell:
- The reserve price is not shown during the auction; if your bid is below it you’ll be notified immediately and your bid appears in the bidding history .  
- If you set a max bid higher than the reserve price, the first placed bid will match the reserve price; if your max bid is lower, we place your full max bid to try to reach the reserve .  
- Sellers can set or edit a reserve price during submission (and lower/remove it once in auction within rules)  .

Read more in the Help centre: I'd like to place a bid on an object with a reserve price. What does this mean and how do I know what the reserve price is? and Can I set a minimum selling price (reserve price)?

input: Ls  Mijn vraag gaat over postveiling nr. 98430827 verkoper wishler. Ik heb hierop geboden en zie nu pas de misleiding vorstenalbum.  Ik had alleen ai samenvatting gelezen. En pas na openklappen zie ik commentaar verkoper dat in vorstenhuis alleen gestempelde zegels zitten. De ongestempelde zijn dus fake!  Sorry...dit noem ik misleiding en wens dus dat mijn bod hierop wordt ingetrokken.  Hartelijke groet Ruud Hamelinck
ai_suggestion: Het spijt ons te horen dat je je misleid voelt door de veiling. Bij Catawiki is elke bieding bindend, wat betekent dat je een bod niet kunt intrekken of annuleren nadat het is geplaatst. Dit geldt zowel voor reguliere biedingen als automatische biedingen.  Als je denkt dat er sprake is van misleiding of dat de objectbeschrijving niet klopt, raden we aan om contact op te nemen met de klantenservice van Catawiki voor verdere ondersteuning. Zij kunnen je helpen bij het onderzoeken van de situatie en het vinden van een mogelijke oplossing.  Voor meer informatie over biedingen en de voorwaarden, kun je de [Buyer Terms of Use](https://www.catawiki.com/en/pages/p/buyer-terms) raadplegen.
gpt4_output: We're sorry to hear you feel misled by the auction. At Catawiki, every bid is binding, meaning you cannot retract or cancel a bid after it has been placed. This applies to both regular and automated bids. If you believe there has been misrepresentation or that the item description is incorrect, we recommend contacting Catawiki customer service for further assistance. They can help you investigate the situation and find a possible solution. For more information about bidding and the terms and conditions, please consult the Buyer Terms of Use.
expected_output: transactional-question
user_question: My question is about auction number 98430827, seller Wishler. I bid on this and only now noticed the misleading "Vorstenalbum" (Prince Album). I had only read the summary. Only after opening it did I see the seller's comment that "Vorstenhuis" only contains canceled stamps. The uncancelled ones are therefore fake! Sorry... I call this a scam and therefore request that my bid be withdrawn. Kind regards, Ruud Hamelinck
