input: Hola , estoy aun esperando devolucion del pedido no entregado por rotura,me dicen que aran devolucion y cuando la van hacer, espero una respuesta,
ai_suggestion: Parece que estás esperando una devolución debido a un pedido no entregado por rotura. Aquí te explicamos cómo funciona el proceso de devolución en Catawiki:  - **Devolución del precio de compra**: Si el objeto se devuelve al vendedor dentro de los 14 días posteriores a la aprobación de tu solicitud y el objeto llega al vendedor en las mismas condiciones en que lo recibiste, se te reembolsará el precio de compra, así como los costos de entrega iniciales.  - **Proceso de devolución**: El objeto, incluidos todos los accesorios relacionados que se entregaron con él, debe devolverse al vendedor dentro de los 14 días posteriores a la aprobación de tu solicitud. Es recomendable devolver el objeto en su estado y embalaje original.  - **Costos de envío de devolución**: Serás responsable de los costos de envío o transporte de devolución del objeto.  Para más detalles sobre el proceso de devolución, puedes consultar el artículo [Refund of purchase price](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-order-issues/i-want-to-cancel-my-purchase) en el centro de ayuda de Catawiki.
gpt4_output: It appears you are expecting a refund due to an undelivered order caused by breakage. Here's how the returns process works on Catawiki: - **Refund of purchase price**: If the item is returned to the seller within 14 days of your request being approved, and the item arrives in the same condition as when you received it, you will be refunded the purchase price, as well as the initial delivery costs. - **Return process**: The item, including all related accessories delivered with it, must be returned to the seller within 14 days of your request being approved. It is recommended to return the item in its original condition and packaging. - **Return shipping costs**: You will be responsible for the return shipping or transport costs. For more details on the returns process, please refer to the article [Refund of purchase price](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-order-issues/i-want-to-cancel-my-purchase) in the Catawiki Help Center.
expected_output: transactional-question
user_question: Hello, I'm still waiting for a refund for an order that wasn't delivered due to breakage. They tell me they'll issue a refund, but when will they do it? I'm waiting for a response.

input: Misleiding bij bieden op object 98430827
ai_suggestion: Het spijt ons te horen dat je een probleem hebt ervaren met het bieden op een object. Bij Catawiki zijn alle biedingen bindend, en we streven ernaar om een eerlijke en transparante veilingomgeving te bieden. Als er sprake is van misleiding of een technische fout, kunnen we biedingen verwijderen of een veiling annuleren. Dit kan bijvoorbeeld gebeuren als een bieder een fout maakt bij het invoeren van hun bod of als er technische problemen zijn.  Als je denkt dat er sprake is van misleiding, raden we aan om contact op te nemen met onze klantenservice voor verdere ondersteuning. Je kunt ook meer lezen over onze [veilingregels en procedures](https://www.catawiki.com/nl/help) op onze website.
gpt4_output: We're sorry to hear you've experienced a problem bidding on an item. At Catawiki, all bids are binding, and we strive to provide a fair and transparent auction environment. If there is evidence of fraud or a technical error, we may remove bids or cancel an auction. This can happen, for example, if a bidder makes a mistake while entering their bid or if there are technical issues. If you believe there has been fraud, we recommend contacting our customer service for further assistance. You can also read more about our auction rules and procedures on our website.

input: pues la  rotura   hace mas de 20 dias que paso y no se nada
ai_suggestion: Parece que ha pasado un tiempo desde que ocurrió la rotura y no has recibido actualizaciones. Aquí te dejamos algunos pasos que podrías seguir:  - **Contactar al vendedor**: Si no lo has hecho ya, intenta comunicarte con el vendedor a través del sistema de mensajería interna de Catawiki para discutir el problema y buscar una solución. - **Proveer evidencia**: Asegúrate de tener fotos o videos que muestren claramente el daño. Esto puede ayudar a acelerar el proceso de resolución. - **Revisar el estado de la reclamación**: Si la reclamación ya está en proceso, verifica si el estado de tu objeto ha cambiado a "disputado". Esto significa que el pago está temporalmente pausado mientras se resuelve el problema. - **Plazo para reclamaciones**: Es importante reportar cualquier problema dentro de los 3 días posteriores a la recepción del objeto para que Catawiki pueda pausar el pago al vendedor mientras se investiga.  Para más detalles, puedes consultar el artículo [The status of my object changed to 'disputed', what does that mean?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/claims-cancellations/the-status-of-my-lot-changed-to-'disputed'-what-does-that-mean).
gpt4_output: It seems it's been a while since the breakage occurred and you haven't received any updates. Here are some steps you could take: - **Contact the seller**: If you haven't already, try contacting the seller through Catawiki's internal messaging system to discuss the issue and find a solution. - **Provide evidence**: Make sure you have photos or videos that clearly show the damage. This can help speed up the resolution process. - **Check the claim status**: If the claim is already in process, check if the status of your item has changed to "disputed". This means that payment is temporarily paused while the issue is resolved. - **Deadline for claims**: It's important to report any problems within 3 days of receiving the item so that Catawiki can pause payment to the seller while it's investigated. For more details, you can consult the article [The status of my object changed to 'disputed', what does that mean?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/claims-cancellations/the-status-of-my-lot-changed-to-'disputed'-what-does-that-mean).
expected_output:
Because the break happened more than 20 days after delivery, the main claim windows have passed. Buyers must report damage or ‘not as described’ issues within three days of delivery to pause payment and let us investigate; if we don’t hear within three days the seller is paid and support options may be limited . Shipping companies usually require damage claims within seven days and smart‑shipping insurance claims must start within 14 days of delivery  .

Try these steps to resolve the case:
- Message the seller from the order page and explain the situation; many issues are resolved directly .
- Gather clear evidence: photos of the full object, close‑ups, packaging and label — at least four photos, plus a short description .
- If you had smart shipping insurance, check and start a claim with XCover as soon as possible .
- If you’d like us to review, use Get support on the order page to submit your evidence .

Read more in the Help Centre: [What should I do if my lot has been delivered damaged?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-order-issues/what-should-i-do-if-my-lot-has-been-delivered-damaged) or [My object wasn’t as described. What should I do?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/most-asked/my-object-wasnt-as-described-what-should-i-do).

input: Misleiding bij bieden op object 98430827  Dit object loopt nog. Ik wil mijn bod intrekken
ai_suggestion: Het spijt ons te horen dat je je bod wilt intrekken. Bij Catawiki zijn alle biedingen bindend. Dit betekent dat je je bod niet kunt wijzigen, intrekken of herroepen. We raden aan om altijd goed te controleren of je het juiste bedrag hebt ingevoerd voordat je een bod plaatst, vooral bij last-minute biedingen.  Als je denkt dat er sprake is van misleiding of als het object niet is zoals beschreven, kun je contact opnemen met de verkoper via ons berichten systeem om het probleem te bespreken. Als dat niet helpt, kun je ons helpen om het probleem verder te onderzoeken.  Voor meer informatie over het bieden en de regels, kun je onze [bieden](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buying/bidding) pagina bekijken.
gpt4_output: We're sorry to hear you want to withdraw your bid. At Catawiki, all bids are binding. This means you cannot change, withdraw, or revoke your bid. We recommend always double-checking that you've entered the correct amount before placing a bid, especially with last-minute bids. If you suspect fraud or if the item is not as described, you can contact the seller through our message system to discuss the issue. If that doesn't help, you can help us investigate the matter further. For more information about bidding and the rules, please visit our bidding page.

input: Volevo sapere quanto tempo devo aspettare ancora per avere una valutazione dall’esperto
ai_suggestion: Di solito, il nostro team di esperti cerca di completare la valutazione di un oggetto entro una settimana dalla sua presentazione. Tuttavia, questo tempo può variare a seconda della categoria in cui l'oggetto è stato presentato. Ad esempio, per aste tematiche o molto popolari, la valutazione potrebbe richiedere un po' più di tempo. Ti informeremo non appena l'oggetto sarà stato approvato da un esperto.  Per ulteriori dettagli, puoi consultare l'articolo [Quando verrà valutato il mio oggetto inviato?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/experts-and-evaluation/i've-submitted-a-lot-for-auction-when-will-it-be-reviewed).
gpt4_output: Our team of experts typically aims to complete the valuation of an item within a week of its submission. However, this time may vary depending on the category in which the item is submitted. For example, for thematic or very popular auctions, the valuation may take slightly longer. We'll let you know as soon as the item has been approved by an expert. For more details, please see the article [When will my submitted item be valued?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/experts-and-evaluation/i've-submitted-a-lot-for-auction-when-will-it-be-reviewed).
expected_output:
Our experts aim to review submitted objects as quickly as possible and generally complete the review within a week of submission, though this can vary by category — themed or very popular auctions may take a little longer. You’ll be notified by email as soon as an expert has approved your object, so there’s no separate waiting-step to track elsewhere .

Practical steps you can take while you wait:
- Check the status of your submission in your submissions area to see if it’s ‘under review’ or requires adjustments .  
- If you left a message for the expert during submission, they’ll read and reply as part of their review — responses typically take one working day to one week .  
- If the expert requests changes, update and resubmit the object so the review can continue promptly .

Read more in the Help Centre: [When will my submitted object be reviewed?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/experts-and-evaluation/i've-submitted-a-lot-for-auction-when-will-it-be-reviewed) and [How do I contact an expert?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/experts-and-evaluation/how-do-i-contact-an-expert) for detailed timings and contact options.

ai_suggestion: Il semble que vous n'ayez pas reçu votre paiement. Voici quelques étapes à suivre pour résoudre ce problème :  - **Vérifiez votre compte bancaire** : Assurez-vous que vos coordonnées bancaires sont correctes dans votre compte Catawiki. Vous pouvez les mettre à jour en vous connectant à votre compte, en allant dans "Paramètres", puis en cliquant sur "Paiement".  - **Délai de traitement** : Notez que le temps nécessaire pour que les fonds soient disponibles dépend de votre banque ou institution financière.  - **Suspension de paiement** : Votre paiement peut être suspendu si un tiers nous informe d'un problème avec la vente de votre objet, ou si vous ne remplissez pas vos obligations envers Catawiki et/ou un acheteur.  Pour plus d'informations, vous pouvez consulter l'article [I made the payment through Multibanco but did not receive any payment confirmation yet](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-payment-issues/i-made-the-payment-through-multibanco-but-did-not-receive-any-payment-confirmation-yet) pour des conseils spécifiques sur les paiements non confirmés.
gpt4_output: It seems you haven't received your payment. Here are some steps to resolve this issue: - **Check your bank account**: Make sure your bank details are correct in your Catawiki account. You can update them by logging into your account, going to "Settings," and then clicking on "Payment." - **Processing time**: Please note that the time it takes for funds to become available depends on your bank or financial institution. - **Payment suspension**: Your payment may be suspended if a third party informs us of a problem with the sale of your item, or if you fail to fulfill your obligations to Catawiki and/or a buyer. For more information, you can consult the article [I made the payment through Multibanco but did not receive any payment confirmation yet](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-payment-issues/i-made-the-payment-through-multibanco-but-did-not-receive-any-payment-confirmation-yet) for specific advice on unconfirmed payments.

For example, there may be an open claim (payments are paused until it’s resolved), your bank account details may be incomplete/incorrect, or the shipping status hasn’t been updated to ‘shipped’ or ‘picked up’. In some cases, a cancelled sale can also lead to an adjustment on a future payout. 

To move things forward, check the most common causes first:  
- Confirm your bank account details are complete and correct in your payment settings   
- Check whether there’s an open claim for the object (you’ll have received an email)   
- Make sure the order’s shipping status is set to ‘shipped’ or ‘picked up’   
- Review whether you still need to finish setting up payments (additional documentation may be required)   
If you mean the buyer hasn’t paid, buyers must pay within three days; we send reminders and may offer the object to other bidders.

input: Buongiorno prima di mettere in vendita un oggetto mi piacerebbe saperne di piu sullo stesso e avere un idea del suo valore per decidere se mettere o meno un prezzo di riserva. Come si può procedere? Grazie
ai_suggestion: Per ottenere una valutazione del tuo oggetto prima di metterlo in vendita su Catawiki, ecco alcuni passaggi utili:  - **Valutazione da parte degli esperti**: Catawiki non offre un servizio di valutazione gratuito. Tuttavia, se proponi un oggetto di valore elevato per l'asta e viene approvato, un esperto fornirà una "stima dell'esperto" visibile sulla pagina del tuo oggetto durante l'asta.  - **Prezzo di riserva**: Puoi impostare un prezzo di riserva per oggetti che hanno un ricavo minimo previsto di €200. Questo prezzo è un minimo nascosto sotto il quale non sei disposto a vendere il tuo oggetto. Se il prezzo di riserva non viene raggiunto, l'oggetto non sarà venduto.  - **Fattori di valutazione**: Gli esperti considerano vari fattori per stabilire una stima, come l'artista o il produttore, il paese di origine, la provenienza, i materiali, le dimensioni, la rarità, il tipo, la condizione e la qualità.  Per ulteriori dettagli, puoi consultare l'articolo [Can Catawiki provide an estimated value of a lot?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/estimates-and-reserve-prices/can-catawiki-provide-an-estimated-value-of-a-lot) e [Can I set a minimum selling price (reserve price)?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/estimates-and-reserve-prices/can-i-set-a-minimum-selling-price-reserve-price).
gpt4_output: To get a valuation for your item before listing it on Catawiki, here are some helpful steps: - **Expert Valuation**: Catawiki does not offer a free valuation service. However, if you submit a high-value item for auction and it is approved, an expert will provide an "expert estimate" visible on your item's listing during the auction. - **Reserve Price**: You can set a reserve price for items with a minimum expected revenue of €200. This price is a hidden minimum below which you are not willing to sell your item. If the reserve price is not met, the item will not be sold. - **Valuation Factors**: Experts consider various factors when establishing an estimate, such as the artist or maker, country of origin, provenance, materials, size, rarity, type, condition, and quality. For further details, you can consult the article [Can Catawiki provide an estimated value of a lot?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/estimates-and-reserve-prices/can-catawiki-provide-an-estimated-value-of-a-lot) and [Can I set a minimum selling price (reserve price)?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/estimates-and-reserve-prices/can-i-set-a-minimum-selling-price-reserve-price).
expected_output:
Catawiki does not provide a free valuation service; however, if you submit a higher-value object and it’s approved, an expert will provide an ‘Expert’s estimate’ that appears on the object page during the auction . When you prepare a submission you can enter an estimated selling price for the expert to review, and objects with an estimated value of €200 or more may include an optional reserve price set in consultation with the expert .

To improve the information experts use and get a better idea of value, follow these steps:
- Take high-quality photos showing maker’s marks, close-ups and any damage; include certificates or lab reports when applicable .
- Write a clear description with provenance, maker, dimensions and condition .
- Enter your estimated selling price in the submission so the expert can advise on a realistic reserve price.
- Consider an independent appraiser for a formal valuation, since expert estimates are not guarantees .

Read more in the Help Centre: [Can Catawiki provide an estimated value of a lot?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/estimates-and-reserve-prices/can-catawiki-provide-an-estimated-value-of-a-lot) and [How does the submission process work?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/submitting-lots/how-does-the-submission-process-work)

input: Γιατί δεν έχει μπει ακόμα σε δημοπρασία το αντικείμενο μου;Έχω κάνει πλήρη ενημέρωση προφίλ εδώ κ τρεις μέρες
ai_suggestion: Για να μπει το αντικείμενό σας σε δημοπρασία, πρέπει να πληροί ορισμένα κριτήρια και να περάσει από έλεγχο από τους ειδικούς μας. Εδώ είναι μερικοί λόγοι για τους οποίους μπορεί να μην έχει μπει ακόμα σε δημοπρασία:  - **Πληρότητα Πληροφοριών**: Βεβαιωθείτε ότι έχετε συμπληρώσει όλες τις απαραίτητες πληροφορίες για το αντικείμενο, όπως περιγραφή, προέλευση, κατάσταση και φωτογραφίες υψηλής ποιότητας. - **Έλεγχος από Ειδικούς**: Οι ειδικοί μας εξετάζουν κάθε υποβολή για να διασφαλίσουν ότι πληροί τα κριτήρια μας. Αυτό μπορεί να πάρει λίγο χρόνο, ειδικά αν υπάρχουν πολλές υποβολές. - **Αριθμός Παρόμοιων Αντικειμένων**: Αν υπάρχουν ήδη πολλά παρόμοια αντικείμενα σε δημοπρασία, μπορεί να χρειαστεί να περιμένετε μέχρι να υπάρξει χώρος.  Για περισσότερες πληροφορίες, μπορείτε να επισκεφθείτε το άρθρο [Offering your special object for sale](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/faq/offerings) για να δείτε τις οδηγίες υποβολής και τις απαιτήσεις μας.
gpt4_output: In order for your item to be put up for auction, it must meet certain criteria and be reviewed by our experts. Here are some reasons why it may not have been put up for auction yet: - **Completeness of Information**: Make sure you have filled in all the necessary information about the item, such as description, provenance, condition, and high-quality photos. - **Expert Review**: Our experts review each submission to ensure it meets our criteria. This can take some time, especially if there are many submissions. - **Number of Similar Items**: If there are already many similar items up for auction, you may need to wait until there is space. For more information, you can visit the [Offering your special object for sale](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/faq/offerings) article to see our submission guidelines and requirements.
expected_output:
Your object may still be with our experts for review or waiting to be scheduled. Our experts aim to complete reviews within a week, though timing can vary by category and themed or popular auctions may take longer . If you recently updated your seller profile, our payment partners may still be verifying your documents — verification usually takes minutes but can take up to 24 hours (excluding weekends), and new objects won’t be added until verification is complete . Once approved, approved lots are scheduled as soon as possible and you’ll receive an automated message when yours is planned .

Do this next:
- Check your submission status in Submissions (Draft, Submitted, Under review, Adjustments required, Approved, Planned) to see whether the expert asked for changes .
- Confirm your seller profile’s verification status and that uploaded documents exactly match your account details; correct and resubmit any mismatches .
- If the expert left feedback, reply via the message field on your submission so they can continue the review .

Read more in the Help centre: [When will my submitted object be reviewed?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/experts-and-evaluation/i've-submitted-a-lot-for-auction-when-will-it-be-reviewed) and [Why do I need to update my profile to be able to sell?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-account-settings/why-do-i-need-to-update-my-profile-to-be-able-to-sell).

input: Please help me to change my currency to euro for all future bidding purposes
ai_suggestion: To change your preferred currency to euros for future bidding on Catawiki, follow these steps:  1. **Sign in to your account**    - Go to the Catawiki website and log in to your account.  2. **Access 'My Profile'**    - Once logged in, navigate to 'My Profile'.  3. **Select your preferred currency**    - In your profile settings, you can choose your preferred currency. Options typically include GBP, USD, and EUR.  For more detailed instructions, you can refer to the [Can I change my preferred currency?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/bidding-basics/can-i-change-my-preferred-currency) article on Catawiki's help page.
gpt4_output: To change your preferred currency to euros for future bidding on Catawiki, follow these steps:  1. **Sign in to your account**    - Go to the Catawiki website and log in to your account.  2. **Access 'My Profile'**    - Once logged in, navigate to 'My Profile'.  3. **Select your preferred currency**    - In your profile settings, you can choose your preferred currency. Options typically include GBP, USD, and EUR.  For more detailed instructions, you can refer to the [Can I change my preferred currency?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/bidding-basics/can-i-change-my-preferred-currency) article on Catawiki's help page.
expected_output:
You can change your preferred bidding currency in your account. Sign in and go to My Profile (or Settings → Account) and choose your preferred currency from GBP, USD or EUR . To adjust your bidding currency select Change; important: you can only change your bidding currency if you haven’t placed any bids yet — once you’ve bid you cannot switch currency for future bidding .

Steps to change your currency:
- Sign in to your account.
- Go to My Profile or Settings → Account.
- Select Change next to bidding currency.
- Choose EUR and save.
If you have already placed a bid, the bidding currency can’t be changed; you’ll need to continue bidding in your current currency .

Read more in the Help Centre: [How can I update my personal data?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-account-settings/where-can-i-adjust-my-personal-information) and [Can I change my preferred currency?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/bidding-basics/can-i-change-my-preferred-currency).

input: How do I create a Catawiki account?
expected_output:
To create a Catawiki account, select ‘Sign in’ at the top right of the site and choose ‘Create a free account’ or use the ‘Create an account’ button on the ‘Not registered yet?’ pop‑up. You must provide an email address and choose a password (between eight and 15 characters). After signing up you’ll receive a confirmation email to activate your account, and you may be asked to verify with a code sent to your mobile phone  .

Follow these quick steps to finish registration:
- Open ‘Sign in’ and choose ‘Create a free account’.
- Enter your email, choose a secure password (8–15 characters), and fill in your name.
- Click the confirmation link sent to your email to activate your account.
- If prompted, enter the SMS verification code to complete verification.
- Keep your details accurate so you can bid or sell later.

Read the step‑by‑step Help Centre article for full details: [How do I register?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-registering/how-do-i-register)

input: I’m not getting the SMS verification code — how can I complete registration?
expected_output:
The verification code is an extra layer of security for your Catawiki account; you’ll need both your password and the code sent to your mobile phone to sign in for the first time . If the verification process is accidentally interrupted, the easiest way to continue registration is to open any auction and use the pink bar link that appears — this takes you to your profile and triggers a new SMS with a code .

Try these steps to complete registration:
- Wait at least five minutes after your first request, then use the link on the code-entry screen to get a new code .
- Make sure your phone has reception and can receive SMS; if prompted, use the ‘Try again’ option or update to the latest app version .
- If the issue continues after these steps, contact our Customer Support team for further assistance .

Read the Help Centre articles for step‑by‑step guidance: [How do I enter my verification code?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-registering/how-do-i-enter-my-verification-code) and [I haven't received the verification code to activate my account. Could you send it again?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-signing-in/i-havent-received-the-verification-code-to-activate-my-account-could-you-send-it-again)

input: How do I register as a pro seller?
expected_output:
To register as a professional seller on Catawiki, sign in or create an account, then select ‘Sell’ at the top right and choose ‘Start selling’ to begin seller registration . During registration you must declare you’re selling professionally and indicate whether you sell as a legal entity or a sole trader. You’ll need to provide your legal or business name, the displayed seller name, VAT number (if any) and company or business registration number where applicable .

Follow these practical steps to complete registration and what to prepare:
- Create or sign in to your account, then go to ‘Sell’ → ‘Start selling’ to select professional seller status .  
- Prepare documents: legal/entity name, displayed seller name, VAT ID, company registration number or TIN if relevant .  
- If you don’t have a company registration number, or you need to change from private to professional, contact our Customer Support team and have the above details ready . Note professional sellers have additional obligations under our policies .

For full details read ‘Become a professional seller’ and ‘How do I register as a seller?’ in the Help Centre: [Become a professional seller](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/seller-registering/become-a-professional-seller), [How do I register as a seller?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/become-a-seller/how-do-i-register-as-a-seller).

input: What happens after I win an auction?
expected_output:
You enter into a contract of sale when you win an auction: you must pay the final bid, the Buyer Protection fee and any shipping or import costs—usually within three days of the auction ending (or immediately if you used buy now). Once we’ve received your payment we’ll notify the seller so they can ship; payments are held in escrow until three days after you’ve received the object   .

To make the process smooth:
- Pay from your Orders page or the email link within three days and check the accepted payment methods .
- Confirm your delivery address and choose pickup if the seller offers it (pickup should be arranged within five working days) .
- Message the seller after payment to arrange shipment and obtain tracking; keep the tracking info and report any delivery issues within three days of delivery if needed  .

Read more in the Help Centre: [I have won a lot. How do I pay?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/payment-options/i-have-won-a-lot-how-do-i-pay) and [Who pays for the shipping costs?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-shipping-costs/buyer-who-pays-for-the-shipping-costs).

input: How should I ship an item I’ve sold?
expected_output:
You must ship the object once the buyer has paid and we’ve informed you — normally within three business days — unless you’ve agreed a pick‑up with the buyer. Ship with valid tracking (Track & Trace) and we strongly recommend registered and insured shipping appropriate to the object’s value, because you remain responsible for the object until delivery. Prepare customs documents for international orders and confirm the buyer’s address before sending the package.   

To make shipping straightforward, follow these steps:  
- Confirm the buyer’s shipping address via the order page and prepare required customs info if shipping abroad.   
- Pack the object carefully, add provenance or invoices if needed, and insure the package when appropriate.   
- Hand the package to the shipping company, then add the tracking code on the order page and mark the order as shipped. If smart shipping is available, consider using it for lower costs and simpler labels.  

Read ‘When should I ship my lot’ and ‘Do I have to send my lot by registered mail with confirmation of receipt?’ in the Help Centre for full details. https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/shipment-preparation/when-should-i-ship-my-lot https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/shipment-preparation/do-i-have-to-send-my-lot-by-registered-mail-with-confirmation-of-receipt

input: Who pays return shipping and under what circumstances?
expected_output:
Generally, you as the buyer are responsible for return shipping and any insurance or transport costs — for example when you withdraw under EU/UK consumer law or cancel a purchase — as set out in our policies . If the return is caused by the seller (for example a wrong address, incorrect customs info, or the object not as described), the seller usually covers the return or resend costs, and payments may be withheld while a claim is investigated .

To handle a return, follow these steps:
- Check whether the object is not as described and message the seller via the order page; if unresolved, open a claim so we can investigate — report issues within three days of delivery .
- If you withdraw under EU/UK rules, return the object within 14 days of approval; you’ll normally pay return shipping and insurance unless the seller or law says otherwise .
- If delivery failed, the party who caused the return pays the new shipping costs — discuss and agree this with the seller first .

Read more: [Who pays for the shipping costs?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-shipping-costs/buyer-who-pays-for-the-shipping-costs) and [Who pays for the second shipping costs if I have to send a lot twice?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/seller-shipping-costs/who-pays-for-the-second-shipping-costs-if-i-have-to-send-a-lot-twice).

input: What items are excluded from the right of withdrawal?
expected_output:
Under our EU & UK consumer rights policy, the right of withdrawal lets consumers cancel purchases from professional sellers within 14 days of receipt, but some object categories are excluded. Exclusions include objects with prices linked to financial‑market fluctuations (for example diamonds, gold and silver), custom‑made or specially adjusted objects, rapidly perishable goods, sealed items with a broken seal (such as audio/video media, software, trading‑card booster packs, graded diamonds), and tickets or dated experiences. 

To check whether the right of withdrawal applies:
- Confirm whether the seller is a professional seller (look for the ‘Pro’ label) — purchases from private sellers don’t have the right of withdrawal.   
- Check the object description for references to customisation, perishability, sealed status or explicit exclusions.  
- If you can withdraw, you’re generally responsible for return shipping and any diminished value caused by handling beyond proper examination. 

Read the Help Centre articles [Policies: EU & UK consumer rights policy](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-terms/policies-eu-uk-consumer-rights-policy) or [I want to cancel my purchase](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-order-issues/i-want-to-cancel-my-purchase) for the full list and step‑by‑step guidance.

input: How does Catawiki handle import taxes, VAT, and customs?
expected_output:
We may collect import tax (VAT/GST) at checkout for certain international orders; it’s added to the final bid and shipping and transferred to local tax authorities so you shouldn’t be charged again on delivery . Customs fees are determined by customs when the order arrives and are the buyer’s responsibility; deliveries are released only once those fees are paid . You must check import licences and restrictions for the object – these are your responsibility .

To avoid unexpected costs:
- Check the object page for where it’s shipped from and the estimated shipping cost before you bid .
- Expect customs duties and clearance charges if the seller ships from another country; these are payable to customs on arrival .
- If we must collect import VAT at checkout (for example EU orders ≤ €150), sellers should include the correct Catawiki tax numbers on customs forms (IOSS, VOEC, Swiss VAT) so the process runs smoothly .

For more details, read [Do I have to pay import tax to Catawiki?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-custom-fees/do-i-have-to-pay-import-tax-to-catawiki) and [Do I have to pay customs fees?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/fees/do-i-have-to-pay-customs-fees) in the Help centre.

input: How does Catawiki handle my personal data?
expected_output:
When you visit or use our Online Marketplace we process your Personal Data so we can provide and improve our services, complete orders and payments, verify identities, prevent fraud, and meet legal obligations such as tax reporting. We rely on contract, legitimate interests, consent and legal obligations as legal bases, and some processing involves automated checks and AI for security and personalisation .

Practical steps and what to expect:
- Your Personal Data may be processed by Catawiki B.V. in the Netherlands and by third‑party service providers abroad; we apply safeguards for cross‑border transfers .  
- Manage cookies and marketing consents via the cookie banner and your Account settings; you may withdraw consent at any time .  
- To close or delete an Account, we start deletion promptly and aim to complete it within one month; certain records (for example financial data) may be retained longer where required by law .

Read the Help Centre article [Data Protection & Privacy Notice](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/buyer-privacy/privacy-policy) for full details, examples, and instructions on exercising your rights and contact options.

input: Should I use manual or smart shipping?
expected_output:
Smart shipping is usually the easier choice when it’s available: we provide a pre-filled, tracked shipping label, don’t require you to pre-pay the label, and can reduce shipping costs by up to 35%; we also simplify customs and offer optional insurance. Manual shipping gives you full control — you set and buy the label, choose your own insurance and handle customs and any claims yourself.  

Choose based on the object and your needs:
- Use smart shipping if available for lower costs, pre-filled labels and easier customs (make sure you select the correct package size). 
- Use manual shipping if you must use a specific carrier, need bespoke insurance, or ship to unsupported countries. 
- Remember to ship within three working days after payment and refund any excess shipping the buyer paid.  

Read more in When and how can I set correct shipping costs? and Info about smart shipping insurance in our Help Centre.  
[When and how can I set correct shipping costs?](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/seller-shipping-costs/when-and-how-can-i-set-correct-shipping-costs) [Info about smart shipping insurance](https://www.catawiki.com/en/help/insurance/info-about-smart-shipping-insurance)
